do_not_merge.reason: Contradiction test: borough differs (Bronx vs Brooklyn) in description/identity context; both are distinct community boards despite shared name 'Community Board 5'.
Contradiction test: borough differs (Bronx vs Manhattan) in description/identity context; distinct community boards.
Contradiction test: borough differs (Bronx vs Queens) in description; distinct community boards. Queens record represents same entity as entity_queens-cb5-grand-ave-2024.
Contradiction test: borough differs (Brooklyn vs Manhattan) in description; distinct community boards.
Contradiction test: borough differs (Brooklyn vs Queens) in description; distinct community boards. Queens record represents same entity as entity_queens-cb5-grand-ave-2024.
Contradiction test: borough differs (Manhattan vs Queens) in description; distinct community boards. Queens record represents same entity as entity_queens-cb5-grand-ave-2024.

suggestion.rationale: All records are community boards named 'Community Board 5' but descriptions indicate different boroughs (Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens). The borough is a strong identity field per entity kind; cross-borough pairs fail the contradiction test and are distinct objects. The two Queens records (entity_community-board-5-queens-2015 and entity_queens-cb5-grand-ave-2024) share the same borough and identity key 'entity_queens-community-board-5'; one is a lifecycle variant (date-suffixed) of the other, so they merge under the same-object test. No other merges are justified.
